title: Flaky Portuguese Fish Skillet
description: A charred and silky Portuguese dish featuring fish and flaky pastry, cooked to perfection in a comal, this recipe is a hearty and cozy meal that is suitable for all, including those who follow a Buddhist diet
instructions: Preheat the comal over medium heat
Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch
Place the cod fillets in the center of the pastry, leaving a 1 inch border around them
Drizzle the olive oil over the fish and sprinkle with salt, pepper, and parsley
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and diced tomato on top of the fish
Fold the pastry over the fish, pressing the edges to seal
Brush the pastry with a little bit of water and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape
Place the skillet over medium heat and cook for 5 minutes on each side, or until the pastry is golden brown and the fish is cooked through
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it rest for a few minutes before serving
